response.justification: The candidate is a deeply experienced hands-on engineer with 25+ years across web, embedded, and high-throughput distributed systems, but the role is an executive CTO position and the resume shows no senior technical-leadership track record: no evidence of scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+, developing leadership layers, governing outsourced delivery, or board-level communication. AI-agent exposure is limited to MCP tooling and developer-facing agentic infrastructure rather than operating production multi-agent systems in regulated, high-stakes environments, and fintech exposure is a single ~15-month stint over a decade ago. Strong IC profile, wrong altitude for this role.
strengths: Deep distributed-systems experience, including low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic
Current hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic-workflow tooling at re:cinq
Long polyglot engineering career with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and open-source founder experience
concerns: No executive or people-leadership experience — never held a role scaling or managing engineering organizations, a hard requirement
No demonstrated production multi-agent AI systems operating with financial or operational consequences in regulated environments
Minimal fintech-infrastructure depth: only a brief investment-platform role (2013–2014), no credit systems, payments, or compliance experience

key_factors.factor: Absence of senior technical-leadership and team-scaling track record
Limited production agentic-AI and regulated-environment experience
Strong backend and distributed-systems engineering depth
